content:
Two things I'd recommend checking.

a) Have you excluded the app from any android battery optimisations and allowed it to run in the background. This is a common reason for email's not synchronising in a timely manner.

  1. Long press the app launcher icon > app info > battery usage.
  2. Toggle "Allow background activity" to ON.

b) Each folder you wish to sync automatically must have it's sync setting turned on (Inbox on by default).

  1. Open the account list.
  2. Tap the applicable account.
  3. Tap manage folders (bottom of list).
  4. Tap folder's name.
  5. Toggle "Sync" to ON.
creator: platform34

content: Thanks. Turning on syncing for each folder solved the problem. A bit of a tedious process, but successful.

content:
Hello Sharky

No, the scenario you describe is not possible with Thunderbird on Android! The app is built upon K9-Mail and fundamentally different from the desktop app.

You can always propose the idea on Mozilla connect where the community can vote for it. https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android
